home.subtitle: An authentic experience aboard vintage carriages. Travel through National Parks and the most beautiful villages of the Central Apennines.
home.sponsorsAria: Partners and sponsors of Transiberiana d'Abruzzo
home.heroImageAlt: Historic train crossing a viaduct among the green mountains of Abruzzo

hero.subtitle: Designed in the late nineteenth century, the Sulmona-Isernia railway is a masterpiece of civil engineering that connected mountain communities before becoming Italy's most iconic tourist rail line.

timeline.title: Work begins
timeline.desc: Construction starts on the Sulmona-Isernia line. The engineering challenge is enormous: the route must overcome steep gradients by carving tunnels through the solid rock of the Apennines.

timeline.title: The inauguration
timeline.desc: The section as far as Cansano and Campo di Giove is inaugurated. The railway immediately becomes the main transport link for goods and passengers between Abruzzo and Molise.

timeline.title: Destruction and rebirth
timeline.desc: During the Second World War the line suffers severe damage because of the Gustav Line. In the 1950s it is rebuilt and modernized, returning to its role as a vital connector.

timeline.title: The suspension
timeline.desc: Regular passenger service is suspended because of high operating costs. People fear the definitive abandonment of the historic line.

timeline.title: The Transiberiana
timeline.desc: Reborn as a tourist railway thanks to Fondazione FS Italiane, it now offers the magic of travelling on vintage trains through the untouched landscapes of the Apennines.

stops.title: Stations along the journey
stops.subtitle: After the history, the route continues through villages and plateaus: every stop is a moment to enjoy slowly.

storia.subtitle: Each stop is no longer just a description: it opens as a visual, narrative and immersive chapter of the Transiberiana d'Abruzzo. Sulmona is the first complete prototype, and the same language will extend to the rest of the route.
storia.panelBody: The redesign replaces the classic descriptive window with a full experience: editorial hero, chapters, visual cues and a narrative rhythm. Sulmona is the first fully built chapter.

cards.label: Chapter 01
cards.title: Sulmona
cards.summary: A medieval town in the Peligna Valley, famous for its artisanal sugared almonds and for the medieval aqueduct with 21 arches crossing the historic center.
cards.story: Sulmona has very ancient origins. In pre-Roman times the area was inhabited by the Italic people of the Peligni and with the Roman conquest it became a municipium. Today it preserves a historic center rich in monuments, popular traditions, gastronomy and artistic heritage.

experience.intro: A town in the Peligna Valley with pre-Roman and Roman origins, famous for artisanal sugared almonds and for the medieval aqueduct with 21 arches.
experience.body: The town preserves historic churches and palaces and each year hosts cultural and traditional events celebrating its history and local craftsmanship.
experience.quote: Sulmona brings together ancient history, popular traditions, gastronomy and artistic heritage.

stats.label: Traditions
stats.value: Giostra Cavalleresca and Madonna che Scappa in Piazza

chapters.title: Origins
chapters.body: In pre-Roman times the area was inhabited by the Italic people of the Peligni. With the Roman conquest, the city became a municipium and developed thanks to its strategic position between the Adriatic and the inner Apennines.
chapters.pullout: Publius Ovidius Naso, one of the most important authors of Roman literature, was born here.

chapters.title: Patron saint and monuments
chapters.body: The symbol of the city is Sulmona's medieval aqueduct, built in the thirteenth century and formed by a long series of arches crossing Piazza Garibaldi, the main square. Among the most important religious buildings is San Panfilo Cathedral, dedicated to the patron saint of the city, together with numerous historic churches and old palaces.
chapters.pullout: The historic center preserves monuments and artistic evidence among the most representative of the city.

chapters.title: Traditions
chapters.body: Among the most important traditions is the Giostra Cavalleresca of Sulmona, a historical reenactment held every summer in which the riders of the city's districts compete in a tournament inspired by the Middle Ages. Another very famous celebration is the Madonna che Scappa in Piazza, held on Easter morning.
chapters.pullout: Each year the city hosts cultural and traditional events celebrating its history and local craftsmanship.

chapters.title: Flavors
chapters.body: Sulmona is famous throughout Italy for the production of sugared almonds, sweets made of almonds covered in sugar, produced by hand and often arranged in decorative flower compositions. Local cuisine also includes dishes based on legumes such as lentils and chickpeas, maccheroni alla chitarra, lamb and sheep meat, and traditional desserts such as torcinelli and calcionetti abruzzesi.
chapters.pullout: These flavors reflect a rural tradition that combines simplicity, local ingredients and ancient preparation techniques.

chapters.title: Location
chapters.body: Thanks to its position in the Peligna Valley and its proximity to the mountains and natural parks of the Apennines, Sulmona is today also an important starting point for excursions and nature tourism.
chapters.pullout: Its location between the Peligna Valley and the Apennines makes Sulmona a natural stop between the city, the mountains and routes through the landscape.

gallery.alt: View of Sulmona with a historic urban atmosphere
gallery.caption: Medieval aqueduct and Piazza Garibaldi.

gallery.alt: Placeholder detail for Sulmona architecture or craftsmanship
gallery.caption: Artisanal sugared almonds and decorative flower compositions.

gallery.alt: Placeholder for a public square or ritual scene in Sulmona
gallery.caption: City traditions and public celebrations.

rituals.title: Giostra Cavalleresca
rituals.desc: Summer historical reenactment in which the riders of the city's districts compete in a tournament inspired by the Middle Ages.

rituals.title: Madonna che Scappa in Piazza
rituals.desc: Easter celebration in which the statue of the Madonna runs toward the statue of the risen Christ among the crowd gathered in the square.

rituals.title: Artisanal sugared almonds
rituals.desc: Ancient artisanal tradition linked to sugar-coated almonds and decorative compositions shaped like flowers.

closing.title: History, traditions, gastronomy and landscape
closing.body: Sulmona brings together ancient history, popular traditions, gastronomy and artistic heritage, making it one of the most interesting cultural centers in Abruzzo.

cards.label: Chapter 02
cards.title: Campo di Giove
cards.summary: Mountain village in the Majella Park, among beech woods, stone and wide-open Apennine views.
cards.story: At the heart of the Majella, Campo di Giove grew along ancient routes tied to trade and transhumance. Rebuilt after the 1706 earthquake, it now balances historic memory, mountain landscape and the slow identity of the Ferrovia dei Parchi.

cards.label: Chapter 03
cards.title: Palena
cards.summary: Mountain village near the Forchetta pass, close to one of the line's most panoramic stretches.
cards.story: Palena developed around a medieval stronghold overlooking the Aventino valley. Today it combines its Ducal Castle, the geo-palaeontological museum and the wild scenery of the Majella along one of the Sulmona-Isernia's most scenic passages.

cards.label: Chapter 04
cards.title: Roccaraso
cards.summary: The line's alpine threshold and the main access point to Alto Sangro's lifts and landscapes.
cards.story: Roccaraso is one of Abruzzo's mountain tourism icons. On the railway it marks the transition to a higher, brighter landscape, visited in winter for snow and in summer for cool air, forests and plateaus.

cards.label: Chapter 05
cards.title: Castel di Sangro
cards.summary: A center of the Alto Sangro between medieval streets, art collections and strong tourist energy.
cards.story: Born as a fortified passage between Abruzzo and Molise, Castel di Sangro preserves a historic stone center, Santa Maria Assunta Basilica, the Civic Museum and the Patiniana Gallery. Today it remains a cultural and tourist hub of the valley.

overlay.ritualsTitle: What the city puts back on stage
overlay.finalNote: The first chapter is a threshold: from here history gains rhythm, material and landscape.
overlay.placeholderNote: For now this city opens in a compact format, with a narrative summary designed to accompany the journey.

activities.title: What to do during the stops
activities.subtitle: Choose the season and discover what to do in the towns where the train stops, with suggestions tailored to the length of each stop.
activities.note: Indicative durations: we will update the timetable details as soon as they are available.

seasonCopy.spring: Blossoms and fresh air: gentle walks and slower rhythms.
seasonCopy.summer: Long days: look for shade, fresh water and wide-open views.
seasonCopy.autumn: Warm colors and seasonal flavors: perfect for an easy pace.
seasonCopy.winter: Mountain atmosphere: short activities and warm indoor breaks.

info.subtitle: Everything you need to know before boarding this retro adventure through the mountains of Abruzzo.
info.tripadvisorAria: Open TripAdvisor reviews (opens in a new tab)

rules.title: On-board rules
rules.body: Historic trains require care. Do not lean out of the windows while the train is moving, do not throw rubbish from the train, and behave respectfully toward the vintage materials and other passengers.

faq.q: Is the train heated in winter?
faq.a: Yes, all the vintage carriages are equipped with original restored and working steam heating and radiators.

faq.q: Can I bring my dog on board?
faq.a: Small dogs are allowed free of charge inside the appropriate carrier. Larger dogs require a dedicated ticket.
Yes, although they are the original 1930s ones. For greater comfort we suggest using the toilets available at the stations during longer stops.

faq.q: Can I bring food on board?
faq.a: Absolutely. There is no restaurant car, so bringing your own packed lunch is welcome, especially during longer stops in the villages.

empty.title: No data available
empty.dark: The station sensors are sleeping deeply. The weather owl is keeping watch. Try again in a few minutes.
empty.light: The station sensors are sleeping. Try again in a few minutes, the weather bunny is on the job.

error.title: Connection lost
error.dark: The candle has gone out, we cannot reach the sensors. Check the connection and try again.
error.light: We cannot reach the IoT sensors. Check the connection and try again.
